era: Hip-Hop/Production. E=5 -- baile funk fusion, global sound from Seattle, producer-as-artist model. Self-released. Twenty-sixth degree in hip-hop era (134-159).

location.formative: Seattle production scene. Internet-age distribution. Global musical connections through digital platforms.
location.career: Sango. Producer-artist. Baile funk / R&B / electronic fusion. Self-released. Da Rocinha series.

members.name: Sango
members.note: Baile funk fusion. R&B warmth. Electronic space. Producer as primary artist. Self-released. Da Rocinha series.

associated_musicians.name: Beat scene community
associated_musicians.note: SoundCloud, Bandcamp, beat scene collaborations. Internet-age producer community.

associated_musicians.name: Baile funk tradition
associated_musicians.note: Rio de Janeiro. Tamborzao beat. Rapid syncopation. Cultural dialogue.

theory_nodes.concept: Global fusion production -- baile funk from Rio + R&B + electronic = unique hybrid. First Latin American rhythmic tradition in the hip-hop era. Global musical dialogue from a Seattle studio.
examples: Da Rocinha series: named after Rio favela. Explicit baile funk influence.
Tamborzao beat patterns integrated into R&B production.
First Latin American rhythmic tradition in the S36 hip-hop era.
theory_nodes.description: Global fusion. Baile funk + R&B + electronic. Seattle studio.

theory_nodes.concept: Producer-as-artist -- the beat is the art, the production is the voice. Instrumental focus. Minimal vocals. Rhythm as primary expression. Producer lineage: Sabzi, Ryan Lewis, Sango.
examples: Sango: instrumental tracks. Beat-first. Production as complete art.
Sabzi (99, 101, 122, 140, 145): producer serving MC or duo.
Ryan Lewis (160): producer in partnership. Pop crossover.
theory_nodes.description: Producer-as-artist. Beat is the art. Rhythm as voice.

genealogy.relationship: Sango at 159. Producer as complete creative voice.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released continuity -- second consecutive self-released entry (158-159). Internet-age distribution. Direct artist-to-audience. SoundCloud, Bandcamp, digital platforms. The contemporary reality of hip-hop production.
examples: Different models, same independence. No label infrastructure.
Internet distribution makes geographic origin secondary.

cross_references.relationship: SELF-RELEASED PAIR! Two consecutive self-released entries. Community grassroots (158) and global fusion (159). Same independence, different models.
PRODUCER LINEAGE! Sabzi as producer/DJ (140). Sango as producer-artist (159). The producer role in different modes across the hip-hop era.
GLOBAL TRADITIONS! Shabazz Palaces: African percussion (Maraire/Zimbabwe). Sango: Brazilian rhythms (baile funk). Both bring global musical traditions to Seattle hip-hop.
PRODUCTION EVOLUTION! Sabzi's sample-based boom-bap (99) to Sango's baile funk fusion (159). 60 degrees of production style evolution in the engine.
